QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_11

question.text: In the Government Model of NPS, the employer (government) contributes what percentage of Basic + DA?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the Government Model, the employer (Central Government) contributes 14% of the employee Basic Pay + DA, while the employee contributes 10%. **The Trap:** The employee contribution is 10%, but the government matching contribution is 14%, not 10%. This asymmetry is frequently tested. **Cross-Exam Context:** The 14% employer contribution for central govt is also the limit for 80CCD(2) deduction for central govt employees under the new tax regime. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Highlight the 14% government contribution as a significant benefit. It is higher than the 12% EPF employer contribution.
